reels.caption: 🇺🇸 Knockouts create instant emotion. They require no context and no explanation. In an era dominated by TikTok, Instagra
good: Hook is visceral and immediate: combat action + bold red text in first 0.5s creates strong scroll-stop
High share rate (4.1% of views): controversial angle on striker dominance drives debate and discussion
Strong watch-through time (8.6s avg): viewers invest despite intensity—not dropping off early
Bilingual captions expand reach across English and Portuguese-speaking MMA audiences
bad: Save rate is critically low (0.09%): philosophical question at end doesn't convert to saved content—no clear utility or CTA
Reach lags views (67% efficiency): algorithm isn't amplifying this as organic content despite good engagement signals
Meta ending (dopamine scroll frame) is clever but leaves viewers without action—finishes on reflection rather than save/follow prompt
reels.top_tip: Replace the abstract philosophical question with a hard-take conclusion + explicit CTA in final 2 seconds (e.g., "Follow for unfiltered MMA takes" or "Save this debate") to convert high engagement and shares into saves and follows

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 Cinco anos parado fazem diferença? Para o Tailandês, a resposta real vem no segundo round, quando a explosão inicial
good: Strong credibility setup: UFC championship belts and official fight branding establish authority immediately
Decent engagement rate (24.6%) shows strong resonance with viewers who actually see it
Story hook (5-year comeback, Round 2 narrative) creates curiosity and narrative tension
bad: Very low reach (260): hook leads with talking head reaction instead of fight action—less scroll-stopping visual impact
Zero shares indicates narrative isn't compelling enough for organic amplification despite decent engagement
Low saves (1): viewers don't find it valuable enough to revisit—missing value proposition
Caption requires context knowledge (Tailandês fighter background) creating friction for cold viewers
reels.top_tip: Replace hook reaction shot (0-1s) with 3-second clip of actual fight action from the match—higher visual stopping power will drive reach from baseline 402 views; engagement is already strong once people see it

reels.caption:
🇺🇸 The full analysis is now on YouTube.
Marcello breaks down Khabib’s and Paddy Pimblett’s comments to explain why a maj
good: Strong hook: close-up face fills frame immediately for scroll-stopping human connection
Specific athlete references (Khabib, Paddy Pimblett) signal credible sports analysis and attract engaged niche audience
Decent retention: 6.7s average watch time shows viewers stay well past the opening
bad: Zero shares—content lacks viral trigger; viewers aren't compelled to amplify despite decent retention
Only 1 save with 518 views indicates no bookmark-worthy insight or takeaway shown in the Reel itself
Repeated YouTube CTA overlay creates platform-friction; Reels viewers want self-contained value, not constant redirects
12.2% engagement rate is moderate for sports analysis; should be 15%+ suggesting the hook-to-payoff feels incomplete
reels.top_tip: Eliminate the YouTube redirect overlay and lead with the actual insight or most striking reaction shot instead. Show the key analysis moment or surprising take within the Reel itself—don't tease people out to YouTube. Shift the YouTube CTA to end-screen only. The current messaging signals 'this Reel is incomplete'—which kills both shareability and algorithm amplification.

reels.caption:
PG’s Top 5 Knockouts — Part 1 💥
Some knockouts don’t even look real 😳 The first edition is here and we’re just getting s
good: Bold red text overlay on hook creates immediate scroll-stop contrast against black/action background
Professional branding consistency (logo, color scheme, outro frame) builds channel credibility
Series hook ('Part 1' + 'Part 2' teaser) creates follow incentive and subscription loop
Solid watch duration (6.3s) indicates viewers stay through the full sequence
bad: Zero saves is critical failure - knockout content should be highly save-worthy for rewatches but feels disposable
Text overlay crowding at hook (logo + fighter name + 'PART 1' + large red text) creates visual noise instead of clarity
Momentum collapse mid-video - quick cuts to crowd reaction and branding lose intensity instead of building it
Extremely low share count (1) despite decent engagement suggests content lacks memorability/uniqueness
reels.top_tip: Slow down on each knockout impact with 0.5-1s hold + fighter/crowd reaction shots. Viewers watch but don't save because rapid cuts feel like a highlight reel, not a moment worth rewatching. Add drama arc per knockout: setup → impact → reaction.
